title: mldsa sign does not validate level; wrong buffer size on direct construction
description: mldsa_sig_len has a fallthrough: _ => 4627 // level 87. This silently maps any invalid level value to level-87 buffer sizing. The generate() constructor validates level in {44, 65, 87}, so code that calls MlDsaKey::generate will never produce a bad level. However, MlDsaKey fields (id, level) are pub(crate), so any code within the crate can construct MlDsaKey { id: KeyId(x), level: 99 } and then call sign(), which will allocate a 4627-byte buffer, send level=99 to the server, and get back a server error — or worse, a truncated signature if the server accepts it. The issue is primarily a readability/surprise problem: mldsa_sig_len's fallthrough looks like a default but is really a silent lie. What would make it clear: either validate level inside sign() (matching the generate() guard), or make mldsa_sig_len return Option<usize> and have sign() return an error on None. The _ => 4627 comment should at minimum say 'unreachable via public API; pub(crate) field allows internal construction with invalid level'.
